name: Peregrine Falcon
ecosystem: Cliff face and city skyscrapers. Natural: sea cliffs, canyon walls, mountain escarpments. Urban: high-rise buildings.
conservation: Least Concern (IUCN). Fully recovered. Delisted from US ESA 1999. ~140,000 worldwide.

vocalization.description: Rapid staccato kak-kak-kak-kak or kek-kek-kek-kek. 4-20+ notes, each 0.1-0.2 sec. High frequency.
vocalization.character: Staccato family signature of Falconidae. Sharper, more rapid, higher than Accipitridae calls.
vocalization.function: Nest defense, territorial warning, hunting alarm

stoop.distinction: Fastest self-powered movement of any animal on Earth
stoop.mechanism: Folded wings, nictitating membrane, nostril tubercles, clenched-fist strike

taxonomy_note.convergent_evolution: Falconidae NOT closely related to Accipitridae. Falcons more closely related to parrots (Psittaciformes) and passerines.
taxonomy_note.molecular_evidence: Hackett et al. 2008 (Science). Australaves clade includes falcons, parrots, passerines.
taxonomy_note.tomial_tooth: Upper mandible notch in all Falconidae. Severs spinal cord of prey. Absent in Accipitridae.
taxonomy_note.wing_shape: Long pointed wings (high aspect ratio) vs. broad rounded Accipitridae wings.

subspecies_pnw.name: F. p. anatum
subspecies_pnw.notes: Continental, inland and urban, most DDT-affected, fully recovered

subspecies_pnw.name: F. p. pealei
subspecies_pnw.notes: Outer coast specialist, San Juans to Olympic coast, seabird diet, less DDT-affected, larger and darker

ddt_story.nadir: Fewer than 100 breeding pairs contiguous US by early 1970s; eastern anatum extirpated E of Mississippi
ddt_story.recovery: Peregrine Fund (Tom Cade, Cornell). 6,000+ captive-bred birds released 1974-1999.
ddt_story.urban_discovery: Urban reintroduction proved unexpectedly successful. City as better food environment than many cliff sites.

connections.description: Flop (E=6, alt rock, Frontier Records). E=13. FIRST FALCONIDAE paired with melodic alt rock. Hooks meet the stoop.
Rough-legged Hawk final Accipitridae. Family changes at 231: Accipitridae closes, Falconidae opens.
Prairie Falcon (SECOND FALCONIDAE). Canyon specialist. The falcon arc continues.
Bald Eagle (FIRST ACCIPITRIDAE). Raptor arc opened 221, second family entered 231: ten degrees of Accipitridae.
